title: Chantier App
sources: README.md: Core software architecture and 'Minecraft Server' philosophy
quests.ts: Foundational quest specifications for District F
database.sql: Schema for the memos table and technical role permissions
forom-config.ts: Category F (Logiciel) and Action 3 (Chantier) labels
App.tsx: React 19 / Vite 7 tech stack and Pixel reward logic
resume: The quest 'Chantier App' (F3) represents the primary digital construction site within the Logiciel district, located at the intersection of Category F and the 'Chantier' action (3) on the 10x10 Finite Rom Map grid. In Phase 1: The Foundations (V1), this quest is the heavy-lifting phase where the abstract visions of F0 and the collaborative feedback of F1 are forged into a functional software reality. To fulfill the mission of 'Saving human communities and long-term memory,' the forom's code must be built as a resilient, sovereign fortress. This is not just a software project; it is the assembly of a digital container designed to survive for a decade. Technically, the 'Chantier' involves the active development of the core application using a modern stack of React 19 and Vite 7, ensuring high-performance rendering for the spatial 5x5 viewport. Every technical decision, commit, and configuration is archived in the local PostgreSQL 'memos' table, preserving the 'Deep Lore' of the application’s birth. Hosting this construction effort on local hardware like an NVIDIA Jetson is a non-negotiable pillar of the 'Minecraft Server' philosophy, ensuring that the community's functional logic and data remain private, sovereign, and entirely independent of centralized cloud providers. Economically, participating in the F3 quest rewards the contributor with 2 Pixels (PX) and 10 XP, facilitating the healthy redistribution of purchasing power within the node. The 'Creator' role, represented by the yellow alignment, leads this effort, building the tools that will eventually serve 1209 potential users. For pilot projects like the 70 ETS Student Clubs, F3 provides the actual functional environment where club history is documented and protected across student generations. Once the node reaches Phase 3: The Awakening, these construction memos will be ingested by ROM, the local RAG engine. ROM will then be able to act as a technical historian, helping future developers maintain the sovereign code by understanding the original 'why' behind every function. Ultimately, F3 transforms software from a concept into a tangible, permanent asset of community infrastructure. Every brick of code laid here secures the structural and functional integrity of our collective memory for the decade ahead. This is the finalized coordinate for active software building. Mission secure. Forom is under construction. Build our future. Yes. Done.
summary: This quest establishes the formal software construction site at coordinate F3, where the forom's sovereign code is developed and archived as foundational deep lore.
